perturbation.original_text: WHEREAS, STW desires to attempt to deepen the Existing CRA Well in order to make it a producing water well and/or drill an alternate well(s) at its own expense, and to lease CRA groundwater rights from Sections 112 and 71 of COFS property; and WHEREAS, STW and COFS desire to work together for the development of a CRA water supply project within the area that will serve to meet the future needs of COFS and the municipal and industrial needs within the region.
perturbation.changed_text: WHEREAS, STW desires to attempt to deepen the Existing CRA Well in order to make it a producing water well and/or drill an alternate well(s) at its own expense, and to purchase CRA groundwater rights from Sections 112 and 71 of COFS property; and WHEREAS, STW and COFS desire to work together for the development of a CRA water supply project within the area that will serve to meet the future needs of COFS and the municipal and industrial needs within the region.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'lease CRA groundwater rights' to 'purchase CRA groundwater rights' creates an in-text contradiction. The original agreement is designed as a lease agreement for groundwater rights, with royalties and other terms reflecting a lease. Changing 'lease' to 'purchase' implies a transfer of ownership, but the rest of the document still operates under the assumption of a lease. This change introduces uncertainty on the true intention of the parties: Is this a sale or lease? This contradiction directly impacts Section 12, where ownership of the wells is discussed, as it becomes unclear whether STW is purchasing only the groundwater rights, or if this purchase also extends to the wells themselves and what happens at the termination of the agreement in regard to those wells.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 12. Title of Producing Wells. Title and ownership of the Existing CRA Well, the Replacement CRA Well and any additional wells drilled on the Property by STW (the "Additional Wells") shall be transferred to STW by COFS and shall be held by STW; however, upon termination of the provisions of this AGREEMENT that relate to COFS Property, STW shall transfer the title(s) back to COFS upon COFS' reimbursement of STW as necessary under this AGREEMENT.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'lease CRA groundwater rights' to 'purchase CRA groundwater rights' introduces a direct contradiction with Section 12. The original agreement, based on a lease, anticipates the return of the wells to COFS upon termination. However, if STW purchases the groundwater rights outright, as the changed text suggests, it creates ambiguity regarding ownership and the obligation to transfer the title back to COFS upon termination of the agreement. This directly conflicts with the terms of Section 12, which clearly states that 'upon termination of the provisions of this AGREEMENT that relate to COFS Property, STW shall transfer the title(s) back to COFS'. This ambiguity makes the contract unenforceable, which falls under the definition of Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: STW shall pay COFS a seventeen percent (17%) royalty of the price STW receives per 1,000 gallons of CRA water produced from the Property and sold. STW shall also provide to COFS three (3) million GPD of CRA water at no cost to COFS at such time that COFS requests delivery of the water for COFS use. Each payment made by STW to COFS will include an accounting statement providing the number of gallons sold, the price paid by any customer(s), the gross revenues received by STW, and the expenses paid by STW for the sale, treatment, use, and/or delivery of the CRA water. STW shall calculate the amount owed to COFS every one hundred-eighty (180) days ("Accrual Period"), and shall provide required payments to COFS within thirty (30) days after the end of the previous Accrual Period.
perturbation.changed_text: STW shall pay COFS a seventeen percent (17%) commission of the price STW receives per 1,000 gallons of CRA water produced from the Property and sold. STW shall also provide to COFS three (3) million GPD of CRA water at no cost to COFS at such time that COFS requests delivery of the water for COFS use. Each payment made by STW to COFS will include an accounting statement providing the number of gallons sold, the price paid by any customer(s), the gross revenues received by STW, and the expenses paid by STW for the sale, treatment, use, and/or delivery of the CRA water. STW shall calculate the amount owed to COFS every one hundred-eighty (180) days ("Accrual Period"), and shall provide required payments to COFS within thirty (30) days after the end of the previous Accrual Period.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'royalty' to 'commission' creates ambiguity. The agreement repeatedly references 'royalty' payments related to water production. Changing it to 'commission' in Section 14(b) introduces uncertainty as to whether this payment is still tied to production volume (as a royalty would be) or is now tied to sales performance (as a commission often is), or something else entirely. This introduces an in-text contradiction and directly creates conflict with section 20, since the taxation of royalties may differ from the taxation of commissions. As well the changed term affects the general intention of the parties if the contract refers to royalty in other places, commission suggests a service is being performed rather than a right being granted.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 20. Taxes and Fees. STW shall pay all taxes, fees, assessments, and costs associated with the rights, pumping, and use of the CRA water from the Property and other properties in Pecos County. COFS shall bear and pay any ad valorem taxes assessed directly against COFS' royalty, any federal or state income taxes payable on the royalty and on the other compensation payable to COFS under this AGREEMENT, and taxes assessed on COFS' ownership of the surface of the Property and other minerals not conveyed under this AGREEMENT.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'royalty' to 'commission' introduces ambiguity and contradicts Section 20. Section 20 explicitly refers to taxes assessed on 'COFS' royalty,' which is now inconsistent with the modified Section 14(b) that uses 'commission'. This creates a direct conflict because royalties and commissions are treated differently for tax purposes and other legal obligations. This directly falls under misaligned terminology in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Subject to the other provisions contained herein, this AGREEMENT shall be for a term of thirty (30) years from the Effective Date, which shall automatically renew from year-to-year thereafter unless terminated by a Party upon sixty (60) days' written notice after the end of the term.
perturbation.changed_text: Subject to the other provisions contained herein, this AGREEMENT shall be for a period of thirty (30) years from the Effective Date, which shall automatically extend from year-to-year thereafter unless terminated by a Party upon sixty (60) days' written notice after the end of the period.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'term' to 'period' creates a subtle, but significant contradiction. While seemingly synonymous, in legal contexts, 'term' usually refers to a fixed duration with clear start and end dates, while 'period' can be more ambiguous and might imply a continuous span of time. The original text is clear about a 30-year term. The change introduces uncertainty: does the automatic renewal create a new 'term' of one year each time, or does the initial 'period' simply 'extend'? This ambiguity affects Section 3, especially concerning STW's rights to maintain and service customer contracts extending beyond the initial duration. It is no longer clear whether those contract rights extend indefinitely with each renewal or are limited by the initial 30-year 'period'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. Term. Subject to the other provisions contained herein, this AGREEMENT shall be for a period of thirty (30) years from the Effective Date, which shall automatically extend from year-to-year thereafter unless terminated by a Party upon sixty (60) days' written notice after the end of the period. Notwithstanding the 30-year term of this agreement, should STW procure sales agreements with customers for a duration of longer than 30 years, STW shall be authorized to maintain and service such contract(s) to the extent of the water volume(s) purchased, and this AGREEMENT shall not terminate during the initial term of any such water supply agreement with a term extending beyond this AGREEMENT.
perturbation.justification: The modified text, by changing 'term' to 'period', introduces ambiguity regarding the duration of the agreement and its renewals, directly contradicting the clarity previously established. Section 3 explicitly states that 'STW shall be authorized to maintain and service such contract(s) to the extent of the water volume(s) purchased, and this AGREEMENT shall not terminate during the initial term of any such water supply agreement with a term extending beyond this AGREEMENT.' The change makes it unclear whether STW's rights to maintain contracts longer than 30 years continue indefinitely upon renewal or are limited to the initial 'period', creating a clear contradiction and potential legal issue.
